name: Editorial Framed Split
description: A long-form editorial split where a framed serif headline card sits opposite a tall lead image, with a secondary image and a chapter-rich body card below. An optional 4-up project meta strip frames the section above; image captions, a pull-quote, an outcome metrics band, a closing CTA and a methodology footnote let the section carry an entire about/story page on its own.

descriptions.descriptive: A two-row, two-column editorial spread anchored by a framed serif headline card with eyebrow, optional standfirst paragraph, and a right-aligned CTA, opposite a 4:5 lead image with optional photo caption. Below, a 4:3 secondary image faces a base-200 supporting card carrying a serif sub-heading, an italic body paragraph, and an optional chapter-style definition list with mono-caps indices. Above the grid, an optional 4-up project meta strip (Practice / Established / Verified by) frames the section as a single engagement; below it, an optional pull-quote (left primary rule, serif body, mono attribution meta), a dark outcome metrics band, a closing CTA, and a methodology footnote close the case. Every micro-label uses font-mono with 0.22em tracking; headings are text-balanced and scale up to text-6xl on desktop. Reduced-motion users get a static fallback; everyone else gets a 0.08s staggered fade-up reveal.
descriptions.usage: Use whenever a brand, story, or service deserves long-form editorial depth — agency about pages, heritage brand stories, founder letters, studio practices, craft-led service narratives. The optional meta strip, chapter list, pull-quote, metrics band and closing CTA let the section stand in for a full about/story page. Place after a hero or below a stats panel; never duplicate it on the same page with another two-row editorial spread covering the same axis.
descriptions.audienceFit: Established professional services, consulting firms, design studios, architecture and interior design practices, heritage brands, luxury hospitality, private medical clinics, and craft-led businesses targeting educated audiences who value substance over flash. Appeals to clients seeking expertise, methodology transparency, and long-term partnerships.
